title: Phase 2 Synthesis — Two Strongest Routes for Phase 3

analysis.object: T826: Phase 2 Synthesis — Two Strongest Routes for Phase 3 depth analysis

route1_balaban_t775.description: Route 1 (Primary): Balaban + T775. Block renormalization + formal GAGA. Classical PDE approach completed by algebraic geometry.

route2_six_step_duy.description: Route 2 (Secondary): Six-step Hodge transfer via DUY (T818). Completely structural.

route3_three_constraint.description: Route 3 (Tertiary): Three-constraint elimination T817. Mass gap forced by exhaustion.

route4_luc37.description: Route 4 (Quaternary): LUC-37 ring closure forces continuum gap from lattice gap (T820).

two_strongest.description: Strongest: Route 1 (Balaban completion) and Route 2 (DUY functorial transfer). Independent proof strategies.

convergence.description: All four routes give mass gap. The convergence is stronger than Hodge (six routes).
convergence.reason: Four routes vs six for Hodge -- still very strong

t826_synthesis.description: T826: PHASE 2 SYNTHESIS. Four independent routes. Primary = Balaban+T775. Secondary = six-step DUY. Three-constraint and LUC-37 as tertiary/quaternary. All converge. T826.
t826_synthesis.reason: Four routes converge on mass gap

analysis.theorem: T826: Phase 2 Synthesis — Two Strongest Routes for Phase 3 (S1105).

key_theorem: T826: Phase 2 Synthesis — Two Strongest Routes for Phase 3 (S1105).
